commit.message:
v0.3.1: Reduce false escalations and improve action suggestions

Tuned system prompt to classify repeat 404 scanners as low severity
(prevents unnecessary Haiku escalations that cost ~$0.005 each). Added
repeat scanner rule so persistent failed scans stay low unless behavior
changes. Updated action rules to suggest watch_ip/rate_limit_ip for all
scanning IPs, not just medium+, so users always get actionable commands.

commit.message:
fix(core, desktop): launch env isolation, imported sessions, macOS permissions (#43)

* chore: use ~/.mainframe_dev data dir for development

Separate dev data directory from production (~/.mainframe) to prevent
interference when running both simultaneously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core, desktop): isolate launch process env and restore state on reload

- Replace blocklist env strategy with allowlist in cleanEnv() — only
  essential OS/user vars (PATH, HOME, SHELL, LANG, etc.) are passed to
  launched processes. Prevents NODE_ENV=production leak from Electron
  that broke Vite dev mode (_jsxDEV not a function).
- Fix fetchLaunchStatuses response parsing — was treating the outer
  { statuses, tunnelUrls } object as the statuses map, so launch state
  was never restored after CMD+R reload.
- Add whitespace-pre-wrap and select-text to console output so newlines
  render correctly and text can be highlighted/copied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core, desktop): imported sessions, plan paths, ghost mentions, macOS permissions

- Remap chatId on history messages loaded from disk — loadHistory embeds
  the Claude session UUID, not the Mainframe chat ID
- Filter ghost entries from sessions-index.json — verify JSONL file
  exists on disk before listing importable sessions
- Resolve plan file paths to absolute — CLI stores relative paths in
  toolUseResult.filePath, causing "Failed to load file" in the editor
- Allow file reads from ~/.claude/ via resolveClaudeConfigPath fallback
  in the files route (plan files live outside the project root)
- Skip mention extraction on command/skill injection messages to prevent
  false @-mentions from template text (e.g. @scope/pkg in /launch-config)
- Suppress macOS Apple Music/camera/mic permission prompts by nulling
  electron-builder's default media plist keys and adding a session-level
  permission request handler that denies media/sensor access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): add developer toolchain vars to launch env allowlist

JAVA_HOME, ANDROID_HOME, GOPATH, CARGO_HOME, RUSTUP_HOME, PYENV_ROOT,
NVM_DIR, VOLTA_HOME, BUN_INSTALL, DENO_DIR, DOTNET_ROOT, GRADLE_HOME,
MAVEN_HOME, M2_HOME — needed for ./gradlew, Go, Rust, Python, etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core, desktop): resolve login-shell env and relative executables

- Replace resolveShellPath() with resolveShellEnv() — runs `env` in the
  login shell to capture JAVA_HOME, SDKMAN vars, ANDROID_HOME, etc.
  that Electron's GUI-launched process.env doesn't include
- Resolve relative executables (./gradlew, ./mvnw) against the project
  directory before spawning — Node's spawn only searches PATH, not cwd

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): add access() mock to external sessions test

The access() check added to skip ghost index entries needs a
corresponding mock so tests with sessions-index.json pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix Phase 2 executor placing zero trades due to missing pending_addition

calculateFinalPortfolio in net-trade-calculator.mjs queried investment_amount
from DB but never read pending_addition. For new bots (investment_amount = 0,
all capital in pending_addition), this produced totalInvestmentDollars = 0 →
empty finalPortfolio → calculateNetTrades returns 0 → "No trades needed".

Fix 1 (net-trade-calculator.mjs): Add pending_addition to the SQL SELECT and
apply it to dollarAmount before weighting — same logic as phase2-execution.mjs
already uses when computing targetAmount for the portfolio display.

Fix 2 (phase2-execution.mjs): When clearing pending_addition after execution,
also absorb it into investment_amount (SET investment_amount = investment_amount
+ pending_addition, pending_addition = 0) so subsequent runs use the correct
base amount. Same pattern as the fix previously made to trading-scheduler.mjs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add lattice split spec — separating engine from live grove (#1454)

* docs: add lattice split spec — separating engine from live grove

Architectural specification for splitting libs/engine into two clear layers:
- @autumnsgrove/lattice (framework: UI, utils, errors, feature flags, etc.)
- @autumnsgrove/grove (domain: server, db, curios, heartwood, payments, etc.)

Covers the split line, 6 surgery points where framework imports domain code,
a phased execution plan (untangle → create → move → migrate → verify),
and the new apps/grove/ tenant-facing deployment.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CDUQEjfrB6ienQNN57ty5k

* docs: update lattice split spec with Trellis naming and new findings

Major updates after rebasing onto main (2-3 weeks of changes):

- Renamed domain lib from @autumnsgrove/grove to @autumnsgrove/trellis
- Classified 5 new modules: reverie, sentinel, durable-objects, git, types
- Noted foliage/ bridge removal (already separate as libs/foliage)
- Added Prism extraction as prior art for the split pattern
- Discovered new surgery points: ui/ → curios/ expanded (artifact
  renderers, CurioPoll), ui/ → server/ (Friend type), errors/ test
  → heartwood/, lumen/ → server/ (resolves naturally)
- Updated module count: 21 domain modules to move (was 17)
- Added changelog section tracking spec evolution

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CDUQEjfrB6ienQNN57ty5k

* docs: rename Trellis to Aspen in lattice split spec

Lattice is the engine, Aspen is the grove. Updated all references,
package names (@autumnsgrove/aspen), paths (libs/aspen/, apps/aspen/),
and poetic framing to match the new name.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CDUQEjfrB6ienQNN57ty5k

* docs: add Aspen to naming system, workshop, and knowledge base

Aspen is the domain layer — the living tree that grows on the Lattice.
Database, auth, payments, curios, moderation, and everything tenants
interact with. Like an aspen grove, every tenant looks independent but
shares a single root system underneath.

- Added Aspen entry to grove-naming.md (Core Infrastructure)
- Added Aspen card to workshop page with tree-deciduous icon
- Added treedeciduous to toolIcons registry
- Created what-is-aspen.md knowledge base article
- Sub-components: Heartwood, Curios, Lumen, Thorn, Warden

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CDUQEjfrB6ienQNN57ty5k

---------

commit.message:
Fix listPackagesByScope: alias reserved keyword 'scope' in DynamoDB query

'scope' is a DynamoDB reserved keyword, causing QueryCommand to fail
with ValidationException. Use ExpressionAttributeNames to alias it.
This fixes the 500 error on GET /api/auth/me/packages.

commit.message:
security: full hardening pass — rate limits, sanitisation, file upload, env validation

Section 1: app.disable('x-powered-by') — remove Express fingerprint
Section 2: rate limiters updated to 15-min windows; dedicated aiLimiter (1hr/5)
           applied to /parse-resume and /resume/ai-rewrite before general limiter
Section 3: JWT_SECRET length >= 32 enforced at startup; no weak fallback exists
Section 4: sanitize-html installed; sanitize() helper added to candidate.js and
           employer.js; applied to all textarea/freeform text fields in PUT handlers
           and work experience repeater fields
Section 5: SQL injection audit — all queries use parameterised pool.execute(); no changes needed
Section 6: /dpr/:postId/resume ownership verified via getProfileId(req.user.user_id)
           (already present); confirmed all profile routes tied to JWT user_id
Section 7: magic-login and accept-invite SQL now filter AND used_at IS NULL AND
           expires_at > NOW() — DB enforces expiry, not just application code
Section 8: /dpr/:postId/resume now checks RESUME_ALLOWED_TYPES (PDF/DOC/DOCX);
           filename sanitized (strip non-alphanumeric chars) in both resume routes
Section 9: config/db.js connectionLimit 10→20; connectTimeout: 10000 added;
           DB indexes added in initDB() for otp_codes, magic_links, employer_team
Section 10: REQUIRED_ENV startup check (DB_HOST/NAME/USER/PASSWORD + JWT_SECRET);
            process.exit(1) if any missing or JWT_SECRET < 32 chars
Section 11: global error handler added (4-arg Express middleware) after 404 route;
            all existing catch blocks already use generic messages — no stack leaks

commit.message:
perf: Add 16-entry sector cache to VFS

Caches disk sectors in a 16-slot ring buffer with clock eviction,
eliminating redundant IPC round-trips to the disk driver. FAT
lookups benefit most since 128 entries share one sector. All write
paths invalidate stale cache entries.

commit.message:
Add --snapshot mode with staging-copy approach and security hardening

Snapshot copies workdir to staging before launch; sandbox writes to the copy
while the original stays untouched. On exit, interactive prompt to review diff
and apply or rollback.

Security hardening from audit:
- Preserve staging on signal during merge phase (don't destroy user's work)
- Detect concurrent workdir modifications via manifest fingerprint
- Handle rsync merge failure with recovery instructions
- Enforce umask 077 on staging dir creation (TOCTOU fix)
- Use cp --no-preserve=ownership for cross-user file compatibility
- Preserve hardlinks with rsync -aH
- Warn on large workdirs (> 1 GB) before copying

commit.message:
perf: Use u64 copies in console framebuffer scroll

The scroll function was copying ~3MB of framebuffer byte-by-byte
with volatile accesses, causing multi-second delays. Switching to
u64-sized copies reduces memory operations by 8x.

ci: add one-shot workflow to enable Grafana iframe embedding (#1643)

## Summary
- Adds a `workflow_dispatch` workflow that patches the Grafana
deployment with env vars to enable iframe embedding
- Sets `GF_SECURITY_ALLOW_EMBEDDING=true`,
`GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=true`, `GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE=Viewer`
- One-shot: delete this workflow after successful execution

## Context
After PR #1639 fixed the `.env.local` build issue, Grafana panels show
broken icons due to `X-Frame-Options: deny`. The root cause is Grafana's
default config — anonymous access is disabled, so iframes trigger a
login redirect chain where every response has `X-Frame-Options: deny`.

This workflow patches the live Grafana deployment via `kubectl set env`
to enable anonymous Viewer access and allow embedding.

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge and trigger workflow_dispatch
- [ ] Verify Grafana panels load on console.gostoa.dev/operations
- [ ] Delete workflow file after success

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(desktop): resolve user's login-shell PATH for launch configs

The packaged Electron app inherits a minimal PATH that doesn't include
nvm/fnm-managed node/npm. Spawn the user's login shell to capture the
full PATH instead of hardcoding known directories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: use DAEMON_PORT and VITE_PORT env vars, env overrides config.json

- Daemon reads DAEMON_PORT (no fallback to PORT) for its server port
- Vite reads VITE_PORT (no fallback to PORT) for the dev server port
- Config precedence: env vars > config.json > hardcoded defaults
- Documented all env vars in README, CLAUDE.md, and Developer Guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(desktop): filter WS events by active project to prevent cross-project leaks

chat.created, chat.updated, and sessions.external.count events from the
daemon were applied unconditionally to the Zustand store, causing sessions
from other projects to appear in the current project's session list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): resolve login-shell PATH at daemon startup

The packaged Electron app passes a resolved PATH to the daemon, but if
that resolution fails the daemon inherits a minimal PATH missing tools
like npm/pnpm installed via nvm/fnm. Resolve the user's login-shell
PATH directly in the daemon so it works regardless of how it was started.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core, desktop): use `-lic` to resolve login-shell PATH

Switch to using the `-lic` flags when resolving the user's login-shell PATH to ensure the full environment is loaded. This fixes potential issues with incomplete PATH resolution in both desktop and core modules.

Add Insider Transactions section to company page via SEC EDGAR

Fetches Form 4 filings from SEC EDGAR public API:

- Filing list from submissions endpoint, filtered for Form 4
- Parses Form 4 XML to extract insider name, role, transaction
  type (Buy/Sell), shares, price per share, and date
- Shows last 10 transactions in a responsive table
- Summary badge: "Net Insider Buying" (green) or "Net Insider
  Selling" (red) based on last 90 days activity
- Client component with loading/error states, collapsible panel

New files:
- src/lib/sec.ts — SEC EDGAR API client
- src/app/api/insider/route.ts — GET /api/insider?ticker=AAPL
- src/app/company/[ticker]/InsiderTransactions.tsx — UI component

Flip buy order logic: notional market first, limit as non-fractional fallback

Always attempt notional market buy first (supports fractional shares, simpler).
If Alpaca rejects with a non-fractionable error, fall back to a whole-share
limit order at price * (1 + limitPercent%). Removes pre-fetch price step and
the orderType setting — execution strategy is now automatic per ticker.

Also remove unused getPositions import and LIMIT_PRICE_BUFFER constant.

commit.message:
Add lane-based spiral system for concurrent object streams

spiralPattern segments now support 'lanes' array for multiple
concurrent streams. Each lane has step (twist rate) and offset
(angular position). Objects distribute round-robin across lanes.
- Single step shorthand still works (backward compatible)
- Zero step = straight line, positive = CW, negative = CCW
- Enables: parallel lines, dual opposing spirals, mixed patterns
S1L1 demo: CW spiral → parallel lines → dual spirals → CCW finish

fix: use filenameMap for entity slug resolution in KB migration

through extractRecords and map functions. Also fix build-data.mjs
to use slugToEntityId when keying PG records into kb.records
(which are indexed by entity ID, not slug).
